title: Boolean Logic
learning_objective: Define a function that returns True when exactly one of two Bool arguments is True, using &&, ||, and not.
hints: See the booleans part of "Ready, set, go!" in LYAH — Starting Out: &&, ||, and not.
"Exactly one" means at least one is True AND they are not both True.
Try combining (a || b) with not (a && b).
